publications.title: An extensive antigenic footprint underpins immunodominant TCR adaptability against a hypervariable viral determinant.
publications.authors: Nivarthi UK, Gras S, Kjer-Nielsen L, Berry R, Lucet IS, Miles JJ, Tracy SL, Purcell AW, Bowden DS, Hellard M, Rossjohn J, McCluskey J, Bharadwaj M.
publications.executive_summary: A single immunodominant TCR clone targeting the HLA-B*0801-restricted HCV epitope HSK (residues 1395–1403) was structurally characterized in complex with its cognate pMHC to understand how a narrow TCR repertoire persists against a highly variable viral target. The TCR achieves broad epitope coverage by contacting all surface-exposed residues of the HSK peptide, functioning as a "blanket" that accommodates frequent mutations within natural HCV quasispecies and enables cross-genotypic recognition. This extensive antigenic footprint underpins the TCR's adaptability to viral escape variants, permitting recognition of common mutations across HCV genotypes while only rare, fitness-compromised variants fully evade detection. The findings clarify why this narrow TCR bias succeeds despite HCV's high mutational rate and support the feasibility of cross-genotype HCV vaccine strategies that target this conserved immunodominant epitope.
